rulings.Case Title: LONNETTE ROBINSON ET AL VS. LARUE PERKINS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Appointing Receiver
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 9, 2019 line 1. RESPONDENT LARUE PERKINS, PATRICIA ANDERSON, BETTY BOWSER Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Appointing Receiver Hearing Required. Parties to meet and confer and agree on a date for a partnership meeting to address management (1) during pendency of the appeal; and (2) for all future purposes. Parties to exchange proposals discussing potential management, e.g. by one or several of the parties, by a third-party professional manager, by a receiver, etc. Proposals to be exchanged by 9:00 a.m. on October 9, 2019. Copies of the proposals to be filed with the Court, courtesy copies delivered to department 501 no later than 9:00 a.m. on October 9, 2019. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: ESSEX MANAGEMENT CORPORATION, A CALIFORNIA VS. TRAVIS ABERNATHY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Further Compel The Deposition Of Travis Abernathy
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 9, 2019 line 5. PLAINTIFF ESSEX MANAGEMENT CORPORATION, A CALIFORNIA Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Further Compel The Deposition Of Travis Abernathy is GRANTED, subject to the limitations set out in ruling on the motion for protective order. Defendant's objections to deposition questions are overruled. Parties to appear on October 17, 2019 for status report, in the alternative, Plaintiff shall take the motion to compel off calendar by notifying the Court by noon on October 16, 2017. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: ESSEX MANAGEMENT CORPORATION, A CALIFORNIA VS. TRAVIS ABERNATHY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Limiting Further Deposition Of Travis Abernathy And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 9, 2019 line 5. DEFENDANT TRAVIS ABERNATHY Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Limiting Further Deposition Of Travis Abernathy And For Sanctions is GRANTED as follows: (1) Defendant to provide responses to all deposition questions in writing no later than October 10, 2019; this includes, providing a full name of "Rafi" and "Ms. Lewis"; to the extent responses to any questions involve attorney-client privilege, a privilege log to be provided; (2) Defendant to appear for a 2-hour deposition on October 15, 2019 to respond to any follow-up or new questions; if deposition is not completed, deposition will continue daily in 2-hour increments until completed; Plaintiff may appear ex-parte to obtain a trial continuance is necessary to accommodate the deposition schedule; (3) 2-hour limit does not include any breaks or exchanges between counsel; (4) Defendant's counsel is ordered to refrain from speaking objections. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: BAYVIEW PROPERTY MANAGERS VS. MARGUIZA ANNETTE TURNER ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 9, 2019 line 10. DEFENDANT MARGUIZA TURNER DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. The Court takes judicial notice of the Order on Motion for Summary Judgment in action CUD-19-665650. Nothing on the face of the order indicates issue preclusion or res judicata as to this action and the Notice is it based on. The Court takes judicial notice of the exitance of other filings in action CUD-19-665650, but not of their contents. The Court notes that the purported dismissal in action CUD-19-665650 was only of "Does 1 to 10." Had the dismissal been of the entire complaint, without limitations, paragraph 1(b)(6) of the Request for Dismissal would not need be checked and filled out. Specific provision of the request controls. =(501/REQ)
